title: Refs and Custom Hooks

learningObjectives.text: Explain mutable refs using a new example and justify the result.
Explain dom refs using a new example and justify the result.
Explain persisting values using a new example and justify the result.
Explain extracting reusable hook logic using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use refs and custom hooks without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Effects and Hooks** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Mutable refs** → **DOM refs** → **Persisting values** → **Extracting reusable hook logic**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Mutable refs
sections.body: A ref is a stable object whose current property can change without causing a render.

sections.title: DOM refs
sections.body: DOM refs provide imperative access after commit for focus, measurement, scrolling, or non-React integration.

sections.title: Persisting values
sections.body: Ref values persist for the component instance but changes are invisible to rendering.

sections.title: Extracting reusable hook logic
sections.body: A custom Hook packages reusable stateful logic. Calls share logic, not the same state instance.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The ref supplies imperative DOM access from an event handler.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Refs and Custom Hooks connects Mutable refs, DOM refs, Persisting values.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
